info.title: Apollo Service
info.description: Service for searching and enriching leads via the Apollo API

prompt.description: Markdown-formatted prompt fragment describing every Apollo-native People Search filter field. One block per field with type, optional enum values, an example, and a one-line description. Designed to be embedded in a caller's LLM system prompt.

schemaVersion.description: Stable 12-char hex hash of the prompt content. Use as a cache key — same content always produces the same hash, so callers can safely cache by this value.

totalEntries.description: Total number of people matching the filters across all pages.

validationErrors.description: Empty array on a successful 200. On a 400, lists the schema validation errors that prevented the call.

include_similar_titles.description: When false, return only strict matches for person_titles. When true or omitted, Apollo may include similar titles.

q_keywords.description: Free-text keyword search across person and organization fields.
q_keywords.example: machine learning OR data science OR AI

person_locations.description: Filter by person's location (city, state, country). Different from organization_locations, which filters by company HQ.

person_seniorities.description: Filter by seniority level. Apollo's full set: owner, founder, c_suite, partner, vp, head, director, manager, senior, entry, intern.

organization_industries.description: Filter by Apollo's native employer industry taxonomy. Use lowercase values from this enum when the user's industry maps cleanly to Apollo; for niche sectors with no enum value, use q_organization_keyword_tags instead.

q_organization_domains_list.description: Restrict to people at these company domains. Do not include www, @, or protocol.

contact_email_status.description: Filter by email verification status. Valid values: verified, unverified, likely to engage, unavailable.

organization_ids.description: Filter by specific Apollo organization IDs.

organization_num_employees_ranges.description: Filter by employer employee-count ranges. Apollo accepts ARBITRARY 'min,max' integer spans, not a fixed bucket list. Use an open-ended max ('10001,') for "N or more". Pass multiple spans to union them.

currently_using_all_of_technology_uids.description: Match people whose current employer uses ALL of these technologies. Use Apollo technology UIDs with underscores for spaces/periods.

currently_using_any_of_technology_uids.description: Match people whose current employer uses ANY of these technologies. Use Apollo technology UIDs with underscores for spaces/periods.

currently_not_using_any_of_technology_ui.description: EXCLUDE people whose current employer uses ANY of these technologies.

q_organization_job_titles.description: Job titles listed in active job postings at the person's current employer.

organization_job_locations.description: Locations of jobs the person's employer is actively recruiting for.

allOf.description: Filter by the number of active job postings at the employer, {min,max} (hiring-intensity signal).
Filter by employer TOTAL funding raised across all rounds, {min,max} integer USD. Undocumented for People Search but honored.
Filter by the amount of the employer's MOST RECENT funding round, {min,max} integer USD. Undocumented for People Search but honored.
Filter by the date of the employer's most recent funding round, {min,max} as ISO YYYY-MM-DD dates. Undocumented for People Search but honored.

organization_latest_funding_stage_cd.description: Filter by employer's latest funding STAGE using Apollo NUMERIC stage codes (label strings like 'Series A' do NOT filter). Code map (certified via enrichment): 1=Angel, 2=Series A, 3=Series B, 4=Series C, 5=Series D, 6=Series E, 7=Series F, 8=Series G, 9=Series H, 10=Venture (Round not Specified), 11=Private Equity, 12=Other, 13=Debt Financing, 14=Equity Crowdfunding, 15=Convertible Note. NOTE: Seed is code 0 in Apollo but People Search does NOT filter on it (code 0 returns the 'has any stage' fallback), so Seed is not addressable here. See the UNDOCUMENTED FILTERS note. Undocumented for People Search but honored.

q_organization_keyword_tags.description: Target a sector/vertical by employer KEYWORD TAGS (industry-style names). The VOLUME-FRIENDLY way to express 'SaaS', 'fintech', etc. — far higher volume than the free-text q_keywords (verified: q_keywords='SaaS' -> 86 matches vs q_organization_keyword_tags=['software'] -> 128,274). Prefer this over q_keywords for any industry/vertical concept. Undocumented for People Search but honored.

q_not_organization_keyword_tags.description: EXCLUDE people whose employer matches these keyword tags. Use this q_-prefixed form — the plain not_organization_keyword_tags spelling is silently dropped. Undocumented for People Search but honored.

included_organization_keyword_fields.description: Restrict which employer fields q_organization_keyword_tags matches against. Omit to default to ~tags. Verified honored values: tags, name, social_media_description (seo_description is silently ignored). Undocumented for People Search but honored.

organization_trading_status.description: Filter by employer public/private status. Only 'private' and 'public' filter (delisted/acquired/ipo/subsidiary/otc are silently dropped). Undocumented for People Search but honored.

person_functions.description: Filter by the person's job FUNCTION (broad role family), lowercase_underscore slugs. Verified honored: accounting, administrative, arts_and_design, business_development, consulting, data_science, education, engineering, entrepreneurship, finance, human_resources, information_technology, legal, marketing, operations, product_management, sales, support. An unknown slug returns 0 matches (not a 422). Undocumented for People Search but honored.

person_department_or_subdepartments.description: Filter by the person's DEPARTMENT (master_* slug) or subdepartment (leaf slug). Verified honored master_* values: master_engineering_technical, master_information_technology, master_finance, master_sales, master_operations, master_marketing, master_human_resources, master_legal. Leaf subdepartment slugs (e.g. 'sales', 'information_technology') also work; an unknown slug returns 0. Undocumented for People Search but honored.

q_person_name.description: Free-text search on the person's full name. Undocumented for People Search but honored.

person_not_titles.description: EXCLUDE people whose current title matches any of these. Undocumented for People Search but honored.

SearchDryRunRequest.description: Apollo search filters. Used to count matching people without consuming credits, creating runs, or writing to the DB.

IntRange.description: Filter by employer annual revenue as {min,max} integer USD bounds. Do not use currency symbols, commas, or decimals.

DateRange.description: Filter by when the employer posted jobs, {min,max} as ISO YYYY-MM-DD dates.

people.description: People returned for this page. Empty array when done=true.

done.description: True ONLY when every page of this filter set's pool has been walked (true pool exhaustion). A page that returns few/no servable people is NOT exhaustion — done stays false and the caller must keep pulling. Do not stop on an empty/low-yield page while done=false.

totalEntries.description: Total number of people matching the search filters across all pages.

totalPages.description: Total pages for this filter set's pool, clamped to Apollo's reachable window (500). exhaustion happens when the next page would exceed this.

hasMore.description: Convenience inverse of done — true when more pages remain to fetch for this filter set.

SearchNextResponse.description: One page of search results with pagination state. Distinguish true pool-exhaustion (done=true) from a low-yield page (done=false, hasMore=true).

emailStatus.description:
Email verification status returned by Apollo. Null when no email was found.

| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `verified` | Email confirmed deliverable via SMTP check |
| `unavailable` | Apollo could not find an email for this person |
| `extrapolated` | Email pattern-matched from known company format (Apollo UI: "Guessed") |
| `unverified` | Email found but not yet verified |
| `unknown` | Verification attempted but result is inconclusive |
| `catch_all` | Domain accepts all addresses — deliverability uncertain |
| `update_required` | Previously verified email that needs re-verification |
| `user_managed` | Email manually entered or overridden by an Apollo user |
| `null` | No email data available |

raw.description: Full Apollo person payload (snake_case, verbatim). Includes any field returned by Apollo not yet mapped to a typed property. Use this for fields not exposed as typed columns.

SearchNextRequest.description: Request body for server-managed search pagination. The cursor is keyed by (orgId, x-campaign-id header, searchParams) — one cursor per distinct filter set, so multiple filter sets for one campaign no longer evict each other.

SearchFiltersRuntime.description: Search filters. Provide them to create or resume the cursor for THIS exact filter set. Re-passing the same filters resumes from the stored page (never resets to page 1); a different filter set gets its own cursor and walks its own pool. Omit to resume the campaign's most recently used, non-exhausted cursor.

cached.description: True if the result was served from the 12-month cache (no Apollo API call, no cost).
True if the result was served from cache (no Apollo API call).

brandIds.description: Brand IDs associated with this search
Brand IDs associated with this enrichment

key.description: The slug or dynasty slug value for this group

brandIds.description: Filter by one or more brand IDs (rows whose brand_ids array overlaps with this list).

workflowDynastySlug.description: Filter by workflow dynasty slug (resolved to all versioned slugs)

featureDynastySlug.description: Filter by feature dynasty slug (resolved to all versioned slugs)

StatsGroupBy.description: Group results by slug or dynasty slug

apolloAudienceId.description: Persisted apollo-audience id. human-service stores ONLY this pointer.

count.description: Live match-count snapshot for the confirmed filters.

name.description: Short human label for the audience.

description.description: Self-contained natural-language description of the target segment. Drives the agentic NL→faithful-Apollo-filters refine loop.
description.example: Heads of growth and VPs of marketing at US-based Series A–C fintech companies with 50–500 employees.

allOf.description: Apollo People API Search filters using Apollo-native field names. All filters are combined using AND. Start broad and narrow down to avoid empty results.

count.description: Fresh live match-count for the stored filters.

get.summary: Debug health check with dependency status
Get the filter-shape prompt fragment for caller LLMs
get.description: Returns a markdown block documenting the Apollo-native People Search filter vocabulary. Single source of truth for callers that need to instruct an LLM how to build search filters. Deprecated compatibility aliases accepted by runtime routes are intentionally hidden. The schemaVersion is a stable hash of the prompt — cache by this value.

post.summary: Cheap filter test — count matches without consuming credits or writing to the DB
post.description: Validates the supplied filters and calls Apollo with per_page=1 to retrieve totalEntries. Performs zero database writes, zero cost tracking, and never creates a run in runs-service. Designed to be hammered by an LLM in lead-service that is exploring filter variants. Schema-invalid bodies return 400 with validationErrors populated.

200.description: Match count for the supplied filters

post.summary: Get next page of search results for a campaign
post.description: Server-managed pagination keyed by (org, campaign, filter set). First call with a filter set creates a cursor at page 1; re-passing the SAME filter set resumes it (no reset). A different filter set gets its OWN cursor and walks its own pool — filter sets for one campaign no longer evict each other. Each call returns one page of 100 people and advances that filter set's cursor. done=true is set only when the next page is past Apollo's totalPages (true pool exhaustion); transient mid-stream empty / low-yield pages do NOT exhaust the cursor (done stays false, hasMore=true — keep pulling).

schema.description: Caller's run ID — used as parentRunId when creating child runs

parameters.description: Caller's run ID — used as parentRunId when creating child runs
parameters.name: x-run-id

schema.description: Brand ID(s) — single UUID or comma-separated list

parameters.description: Brand ID(s) — single UUID or comma-separated list
parameters.name: x-brand-id

schema.description: Priority audience ID for per-audience cost attribution. Set on campaign-flow calls; absent otherwise.

parameters.description: Priority audience ID for per-audience cost attribution. Set on campaign-flow calls; absent otherwise.
parameters.name: x-audience-id

400.description: No cursor found (call with searchParams first) or validation error

post.summary: Enrich a person via Apollo to reveal their email
post.description: Enrich a single person by Apollo person ID. Uses 12-month cache. If runId is provided, stores record and tracks costs.

schema.description: Caller's run ID — used as parentRunId when creating child runs

parameters.description: Caller's run ID — used as parentRunId when creating child runs
parameters.name: x-run-id

schema.description: Brand ID(s) — single UUID or comma-separated list

parameters.description: Brand ID(s) — single UUID or comma-separated list
parameters.name: x-brand-id

schema.description: Priority audience ID for per-audience cost attribution. Set on campaign-flow calls; absent otherwise.

parameters.description: Priority audience ID for per-audience cost attribution. Set on campaign-flow calls; absent otherwise.
parameters.name: x-audience-id

post.summary: Match a person by name and organization domain via Apollo
post.description: Match a single person by firstName + lastName + organizationDomain. Uses 12-month cache. Costs tracked as apollo-credit (only charged when email is found).

schema.description: Caller's run ID — used as parentRunId when creating child runs

parameters.description: Caller's run ID — used as parentRunId when creating child runs
parameters.name: x-run-id

schema.description: Brand ID(s) — single UUID or comma-separated list

parameters.description: Brand ID(s) — single UUID or comma-separated list
parameters.name: x-brand-id

schema.description: Priority audience ID for per-audience cost attribution. Set on campaign-flow calls; absent otherwise.

parameters.description: Priority audience ID for per-audience cost attribution. Set on campaign-flow calls; absent otherwise.
parameters.name: x-audience-id

get.summary: Get all searches for a run
Get all enrichments for a run

post.description: Returns aggregated search and enrichment stats. All body filters are optional; orgId is always applied from auth.

post.summary: Transfer solo-brand rows from one org to another
post.description: Re-assigns all rows referencing exactly this brandId (solo-brand only) from sourceOrgId to targetOrgId. Rows with multiple brand IDs are skipped. Idempotent — running twice is a no-op.

post.summary: Validate a batch of items against Apollo schemas
post.description: Validates items against the specified endpoint schema (search, enrich, or bulk-enrich).

post.summary: Build + persist a faithful Apollo audience from a natural-language segment
post.description: Runs the agentic NL→faithful-Apollo-filters refine loop (LLM via chat-service, free Apollo dry-runs for live counts), then persists the confirmed audience. Returns the apollo-audience id, the faithful filters, and the count snapshot. The LLM cost is owned by chat-service; this endpoint declares no cost (dry-runs are free).

get.summary: Fetch a persisted Apollo audience by id

post.summary: Re-count a persisted Apollo audience (free dry-run)
post.description: Re-runs the free Apollo dry-run against the stored filters and returns a fresh count. Also refreshes the stored count snapshot. No credits consumed.
